title: OverCR Semantic Memory Record v2.1.0
description: Canonical schema for a single semantic memory record in the OverCR substrate. Every memory record is advisory — canonical filesystem state remains authoritative.

memory_id.description: Unique identifier. Generated as 'mem-' + 8-char hex suffix. Immutable once created.

source.description: Origin of this memory: operator, subagent name, or promotion rule identifier.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of last status change.

confidence.description: Confidence score 0.0–1.0. Set by provenance rule or operator. Never auto-adjusted.

type.description: How this memory entered the system.

rule.description: Identifier of the promotion rule or explicit creation method.

operator_id.description: Operator who created or approved this memory, if applicable.

task_id.description: Link to OverCR task that produced this memory, if applicable.

artifact_path.description: Filesystem path to the source artifact, if memory was promoted from a file.

provenance.description: Provenance chain: how this memory was created and by whom.

tags.description: Searchable tags. At least one required for discoverability.

project_scope.description: Project or domain this memory belongs to. Enables project-scoped retrieval.

semantic_summary.description: Human-readable summary of what this memory records. Not a prompt — advisory context.

path.description: Filesystem path to the supporting file.

description.description: What this artifact contains and why it supports this memory.

supporting_artifacts.description: Optional list of filesystem artifacts that support this memory.

path.description: Filesystem path to the authoritative state file.

field.description: The specific field or key this memory observes in the canonical state.

as_of.description: Snapshot timestamp of when this ref was verified against the canonical state.

canonical_state_refs.description: References to canonical filesystem state this memory is derived from. If these state files change, this memory may become stale.

conflicting_memory_id.description: ID of the memory record this one contradicts.

contradiction_refs.description: References to other memories that contradict this one. Populated by MemoryConflictResolver.

status.description: Lifecycle status. active=current truth, stale=may need refresh, rejected=operator disconfirmed, superseded=replaced by newer memory.

superseded_by.description: If status=superseded, the memory_id that replaced this one.

stale_reason.description: If status=stale, explanation of why this memory may be outdated.
